title: ANSI Launches Hydrogen Standards Coordination Initiative and Seeks Input
content: The American National Standards Institute has unveiled its Hydrogen Standards Coordination Initiative to advance awareness and development of safety and technical standards across the complete hydrogen value chain, spanning production, storage, transportation, carbon capture and utilization, and related infrastructure. Phase I debuts a public website and informational webinars, centered on two deliverables: a Hydrogen Standards Landscape that compiles existing standards, related activities, supporting organizations, and metadata through stakeholder input, and a series of educational sessions featuring standards and code developers with opportunities for dialogue. ANSI is inviting contributions via a Request for Information that asks for details on published industry consensus standards, ongoing activities, and guidance documents; responses are requested by March 6, 2026, by submitting the RFI spreadsheet to the designated contact. The organization will host informational webinars on March 16 and March 31, with registration open and a call for presenters. Looking ahead, Phase II will launch in spring 2026 and include a technical workshop focused on preâ€‘standardization research, standards and conformity assessment needs, and potential regulatory framework considerations, culminating in an executive report that outlines key challenges, standards gaps, and recommendations. ANSIâ€™s leadership emphasizes that robust standardization is essential for safety, interoperability, and commercial viability as hydrogenâ€™s role expands in cleaner transport, power systems, and largeâ€‘scale energy storage.
author: Analysts Account
